initiatives.name: Black and Pink SeaTac
initiatives.description: A queer/trans prison abolition collective fostering mutual aid and solidarity through prisoner support and advocacy. They coordinate letter writing, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building power through LGBTQ+ prisoner organizing, abolition education, and creating spaces for connection and support across prison walls.

initiatives.name: Transit Riders Union
initiatives.description: The Transit Riders Union is an independent, democratic, member-run union of transit riders organizing for better public transit in Seattle, King County and beyond.

initiatives.name: Seattle Protest Network
initiatives.description: a volunteer-led citizen journalism effort established to document and amplify the events of the 2020 Seattle Protests.

initiatives.name: Tacoma IWW
initiatives.description: Tacoma, Wa. branch of the @industrialworkersoftheworld

initiatives.name: Got Green
initiatives.description: A South Seattle-based environmental justice organization led by people of color and low-income people. Through mutual aid and grassroots organizing, they work on food access, green jobs, and climate justice while building community power in the South End.

initiatives.name: King County Equity Now
initiatives.description: Community-led coalition working to achieve racial and economic justice through land acquisition, capacity building, and policy advocacy in historically Black neighborhoods.

initiatives.name: Subvert UD
initiatives.description: Community collective based in Duwamish territory. Food distributions every Sunday

initiatives.name: Cold World Seattle
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective dedicated to helping our unhoused neighbors.

initiatives.name: LA CASA DEL XOLOITZCUINTLE
initiatives.description: mutual aid organization, arts & music venue

initiatives.name: The People's Assembly
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization in Tacoma focused on community empowerment and social justice through mutual aid, education, and direct action. They work to build solidarity networks and create sustainable community support systems.

initiatives.name: Superfamilia KC
initiatives.description: Mutual aid group providing support and resources for undocumented communities.

initiatives.name: Single Spark Seattle
initiatives.description: An autonomous group giving food, fuel, and water to unhoused Seattle residents.

initiatives.name: A Will and A Way Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Capitol Hill

initiatives.name: Emerald Sea Food Not Bombs
initiatives.description: Local chapter providing free vegan meals and mutual aid in Seattle.

initiatives.name: Crossroads Food not Bombs
initiatives.description: Hot food, zines, groceries & more! Every Sunday 12-2pm at Crossroads Park 999 164th Ave NE (Bellevue)

initiatives.name: Tacoma Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Tacoma Mutual Aid Collective works in solidarity with Tacoma communities to support resource, knowledge, and skill sharing across our neighborhoods.

initiatives.name: Green Light Project
initiatives.description: Peer led mutual aid and harm reduction for sex workers/drug users in Seattle.

initiatives.name: North Beacon Hill Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: mutual aid & direct service program on unceded Duwamish land

initiatives.name: Blackstar Farmers
initiatives.description: Our mission is to secure the liberation of all beings through land sovereignty, food sovereignty, and self-determination. We work towards this mission through land stewardship, mutual aid, education, and direct action.

initiatives.name: Mutual Aid Books
initiatives.description: free books centering Black & Indigenous authors for the people

initiatives.name: Kaibigan FFPS
initiatives.description: Filipino Food Pantry Seattle building international solidarity through mutual aid and community support for the Filipino diaspora.

initiatives.name: Sông2Sea
initiatives.description: Vietnamese solidarity organization fostering political education and community building through study groups, cultural projects, and mutual aid.

initiatives.name: Capybara Colectiva
initiatives.description: A militant, grassroots org of Latin American diaspora fighting and exposing the u.s. imperialism that forces migrants to leave their homelands.

initiatives.name: Lifewerq Project
initiatives.description: LGBTQ+ and BIPOC-led organization providing mutual aid, community support, and resources for queer and trans communities in Seattle.

initiatives.name: Seattle Student Union
initiatives.description: Student-led organization uniting Seattle schools through collective action, winning resources for mental healthcare, COVID safety, and other community needs.

initiatives.name: People's Harm Reduction Alliance (PHRA)
initiatives.description: A community-based mutual aid organization providing vital harm reduction services and support to people who use drugs since 2007. Through their various programs including syringe exchange, naloxone distribution, and mobile outreach, they work to empower drug users and provide compassionate, non-judgmental care while building networks of community support across Seattle and King County.

initiatives.name: Pipsqueak Seattle
initiatives.description: Pipsqueak is a radical, sober, all-ages event space located on occupied Duwamish land at 173 16th Ave.

initiatives.name: Wa Na Wari
initiatives.description: A Black cultural center and mutual aid hub housed in a 5th-generation Black-owned home, creating space for Black ownership, possibility, and belonging through art, historic preservation, and community connection. They provide community meals, host art exhibitions, maintain community gardens, and offer various programs while working to preserve Black presence and combat displacement in Seattle's Central District.

initiatives.name: The Long Haul Kitchen
initiatives.description: Mutual Aid Group supporting our neighbors in Georgetown and SODO (occupied Duwamish land)

initiatives.name: Estelita's Library
initiatives.description: A social justice community library and bookstore providing access to radical literature, educational resources, and mutual aid through their community space. They focus on Black and Brown liberation texts while fostering community learning, political education, and solidarity through their lending library, events, and pay-what-you-can book program.

initiatives.name: Coalition of Anti-Racist Whites (CARW)
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community solidarity through white anti-racist organizing in Seattle. They coordinate political education, direct action support, and solidarity networks while building power through racial justice education, accountable organizing, and creating spaces for white people to work for racial justice in partnership with BIPOC-led organizations.

initiatives.name: South Asians Resist
initiatives.description: Militant org of South Asians organizing against caste, class, imperialism.

initiatives.name: Decriminalize Seattle
initiatives.description: Grassroots coalition of Seattle abolitionist orgs • holding it down for past 10 yrs • driving #DefundSPD

initiatives.name: Seattle Revolutionary Socialists
initiatives.description: A revolutionary socialist organization fostering mutual aid and community solidarity through collective organizing in Seattle. They coordinate political education, direct action support, and solidarity networks while building power through community organizing and creating spaces for collective study and struggle.

initiatives.name: Feminists for Jina PNW-Seattle
initiatives.description: Intersectional and transnational feminists from Iran in diaspora and exile, in solidarity with the revolutionary people around the world.

initiatives.name: 1312 Press
initiatives.description: Small anarchist zine-distro and publisher

initiatives.name: Mutual Aid South & East King
initiatives.description: Mutual aid support for survivors, sick & disabled, immunocompromised, undocumented, & QTBIPOC folks in South & East King county

initiatives.name: La Resistencia NW
initiatives.description: Our goal is to shut down the NWDC, and to end all detentions and deportations in Washington State.

initiatives.name: Warm Foot Fund Seattle
initiatives.description: Serving our unhoused neighbors on occupied Duwamish land. Specialized commitment to providing resources during extreme weather events

initiatives.name: Homies Helping Homies
initiatives.description: Mutual aid for the homies. Solidarity. Reparations. Awareness. ACAB

initiatives.name: Auburn Mutual Aid
initiatives.description: Food distribution in Auburn, WA.

initiatives.name: Books to Prisoners
initiatives.description: A Seattle-based nonprofit fostering mutual aid through free book distribution to people in prison nationwide. They coordinate book donations, letter writing, and solidarity networks while building connections through literature and education across prison walls.

initiatives.name: Distro Fugitive
initiatives.description: An invitation to revolt...
